summary: This video is an educational/conference presentation on AI application to laparoscopic simulation skills training (SAGES 2022). It is NOT a performance video of an FLS task. The content discusses: (1) AI algorithms for assessing peg transfer and bean drop exercises; (2) Materials and methods comparing AI detection to expert evaluator gold standard; (3) Results showing 93.02% Cohen Kappa agreement for peg transfer and 79.17% for bean drop; (4) Limitations of the current approach; (5) Future projections for expanding to 11 exercises.
cannot_score: This is instructional/conference content with narration, slides, and discussion of research methodology. No individual trainee FLS task performance is visible. Scoring rubric does not apply.

verbal_cues: AI algorithm detects position of grasper-clamps
Compares AI assessment to expert evaluators using Cohen Kappa
Feasibility of developing AI algorithms for laparoscopic simulation training
High agreement levels between AI and gold standard evaluators
teaching_content.research_focus: Validation of AI-based objective assessment of FLS exercises (peg transfer, bean drop) against expert gold standard evaluations
teaching_content.skill_level_demonstrated: Not applicable—research presentation, not task demonstration

limitations_noted: Detection of fallen objects requires improvement
Current application restricted to assessment use
future_projections: Application to other similar programs and simulators
Automated certification of basic laparoscopic skills
